stage.name: Staying Safe in Crypto (Elementary)
stage.description: Scams to avoid, how to protect your accounts, and the safety checklist every beginner needs.

elements.content: In crypto, you are your own bank. That's amazing — but it also means if something goes wrong, there's no customer service to call. No refund button. No 'forgot password' email. That's why security is the #1 skill every crypto person must learn first.

🔑

Guard Your Keys

Private key = your money. Lose it = lose everything.

🧠

Think First

Scammers are smart. One wrong click can empty your wallet.

📚

Keep Learning

New scams appear daily. Staying informed is your best defense.

actions.text: 🛡️ Staying Safe in Crypto
The most important skill you'll learn

In crypto, you are your own bank. That's amazing — but it also means if something goes wrong, there's no customer service to call. No refund button. No 'forgot password' email. That's why security is the #1 skill every crypto person must learn first
🔑Guard Your KeysPrivate key = your money. Lose it = lose everything
🧠Think FirstScammers are smart. One wrong click can empty your wallet
✅VerifyAlways double-check addresses, websites, and senders
📚Keep LearningNew scams appear daily. Staying informed is your best defense

elements.content: Know these — they could save your savings

🎁 Giveaway Scam: Fake celebrity says 'Send 1 BTC, get 2 back!' — it's always a lie.   🎣 Phishing: Fake website that looks real — one typo in the URL and your login is stolen.   💌 Fake Support: 'Support' DMs asking for your seed phrase — real support NEVER does this.   📈 Pump & Dump: Group hypes a coin, price spikes, they sell, you lose.

💌

Fake DM Support

Real support never asks for seed phrase

📈

Pump & Dump

Hype coin, spike, crash — you hold the loss

actions.text: 🚨 Common Scams
Know these — they could save your savings

🎁 Giveaway Scam: Fake celebrity says 'Send 1 BTC, get 2 back!' — it's always a lie.   🎣 Phishing: Fake website that looks real — one typo in the URL and your login is stolen.   💌 Fake Support: 'Support' DMs asking for your seed phrase — real support NEVER does this.   📈 Pump & Dump: Group hypes a coin, price spikes, they sell, you lose
🎁Giveaway ScamNobody ever doubles your crypto
🎣Phishing SitesFake sites steal your login
💌Fake DM SupportReal support never asks for seed phrase
📈Pump & DumpHype coin, spike, crash — you hold the loss

elements.content:
Phishing is when a scammer pretends to be a trusted service — MetaMask, Coinbase, a DeFi protocol — to trick you into entering your seed phrase or private key on a fake website.


Common forms: fake emails, fake Discord support DMs, Google ads pointing to copycat sites. A real wallet or exchange will NEVER ask for your seed phrase. Ever. If someone asks — it is a scam 100% of the time.

💬 Fake Support
DMs asking for seed phrase

✅ Rule
Legit services NEVER ask for seed phrase

actions.text: 🎣 Phishing — The Most Common Attack
How scammers steal wallets by impersonation

Phishing is when a scammer pretends to be a trusted service — MetaMask, Coinbase, a DeFi protocol — to trick you into entering your seed phrase or private key on a fake website. Common forms: fake emails, fake Discord support DMs, Google ads pointing to copycat sites. A real wallet or exchange will NEVER ask for your seed phrase. Ever. If someone asks — it is a scam 100% of the time
📧 Fake Emails. Links to copycat sites
💬 Fake Support. DMs asking for seed phrase
🔗 Fake URLs. Check spelling carefully
✅ Rule. Legit services NEVER ask for seed phrase

elements.content: Two of the most destructive scams in crypto

Fake Giveaways — "Send 1 ETH, receive 2 ETH back." It is always a scam. Elon Musk, Vitalik, and Coinbase have all been impersonated this way. Nobody is giving away free crypto.


Rug Pulls — a team creates a new token, hypes it up, raises money — then disappears with all the funds. The token becomes worthless overnight. Over $2 billion was lost to rug pulls in 2021 alone.

🎁 Giveaway Scam
"Send X, get 2X back" = always fake

🪤 Rug Pull
Team dumps tokens and disappears

actions.text: 💎 Fake Giveaways & Rug Pulls
Two of the most destructive scams in crypto

Fake Giveaways — "Send 1 ETH, receive 2 ETH back." It is always a scam. Elon Musk, Vitalik, and Coinbase have all been impersonated this way. Nobody is giving away free crypto. Rug Pulls — a team creates a new token, hypes it up, raises money — then disappears with all the funds. The token becomes worthless overnight. Over $2 billion was lost to rug pulls in 2021 alone
🎁 Giveaway Scam. "Send X, get 2X back" = always fake
🪤 Rug Pull. Team dumps tokens and disappears
📊 Red Flag. Anonymous team + unrealistic returns
🛡️ Defence. Stick to established, audited projects

elements.content:
Social engineering uses psychology rather than hacking. Scammers build trust over weeks, create urgency, or exploit emotions to get you to make a mistake.


Common tactics: romance scams (fake relationships that end with a request to invest), fake job offers, "investment managers" promising guaranteed returns.

❤️ Romance Scam
Weeks of relationship → steal funds

actions.text: 🧠 Social Engineering & Red Flags
How scammers manipulate people psychologically

Social engineering uses psychology rather than hacking. Scammers build trust over weeks, create urgency, or exploit emotions to get you to make a mistake. Common tactics: romance scams (fake relationships that end with a request to invest), fake job offers, "investment managers" promising guaranteed returns
❤️ Romance Scam. Weeks of relationship → steal funds
💼 Fake Job. Pay "training fee" upfront
⏰ Urgency. "Act now or miss out"
💯 Guaranteed Returns. Impossible in real investing

content.html:
🚨 Scam Spotter

Can you tell the difference between a real message and a scam? Tap your answer.

actions.text: Let's explore this interactively. Use the simulation below to experiment with the concepts we just covered.
Try different inputs and see how the outputs change. Hands-on exploration builds intuition faster than reading.

questions.question: Someone sends you a DM saying 'I'm from OnlyCrypto support — send me your seed phrase to verify your account.' What should you do?

options.label: Send them the seed phrase — it's the support team
Block and report them immediately — real support never asks for your seed phrase
Send them half the phrase to test if they're real
Email them your private key instead

questions.analysis: Real support from any legitimate crypto platform will NEVER ask for your seed phrase or private key. Anyone asking for it is a scammer — block and report immediately.

questions.question: What is a phishing attack?

options.label: A way to mine cryptocurrency using fishing boats
A fake website or email designed to look real so you type in your login or seed phrase
A type of blockchain consensus mechanism
A legal way to earn extra crypto

questions.analysis: Phishing is when scammers create fake websites (or emails) that look identical to real ones. You think you're logging into your real wallet, but you're actually giving your credentials to a thief.

questions.question: Which of these is the CORRECT way to protect your account?

options.label: Use the same password on every site so you don't forget
Share your password with a trusted friend as a backup
Enable 2FA (two-factor authentication) and use a different strong password for every account
Write your private key in your email drafts

questions.analysis: Use a unique strong password for each account, enable 2FA, and never store your private key or seed phrase digitally. This combination makes it dramatically harder for hackers to break in.

questions.question: A stranger online promises to double your XRP if you send them some first. What is this?

options.label: The giveaway scam — a classic fraud where you lose everything you send

questions.analysis: The 'double your money' giveaway scam is one of the oldest tricks in crypto. Nobody is going to double your money — ever. If you send crypto to a stranger, it is gone forever.

actions.text: Time to test what you've learned! Answer each question and see how you do.
Take your time — the goal is understanding, not just getting the right answer.
